mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 9.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration that suppresses corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 9.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansions in real sales of existing homes and housing starts indicate robust consumer demand and construction activity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when both series trend upward, or interaction term Rank IC drops below 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the index of industrial production below 90.0 reflects constrained output capacity that limits revenue growth, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 60% of observations when INDPRO is below 90.0, or the signal shows no predictive accuracy during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: High consumer price index readings above 225.0 combined with a federal funds rate below 0.50 percent create a real rate inversion that triggers equity sell-offs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined high-inflation low-rate regime yields positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction effect Rank IC is insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrate strengthening economic activity that supports corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of months with payroll growth, or the payroll growth signal Rank IC falls below 0.02 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 1.5 percent quarter-over-quarter confirms broad-based economic expansion that lifts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in over 60% of quarters where GDP growth surpasses 1.5%, or the threshold signal fails to achieve a Rank IC above 0.04 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling real sales of existing homes indicates inventory buildup that forces production cuts, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal predicts positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 1-year rolling period.
